content:
NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Embed tops RTEB, but the real finding is that better retrieval cuts your agent’s token bill, not just its errors.

9 min read
Just now

--

Everyone in this field wants to talk about the model, and almost nobody wants to talk about the retriever. Which LLM reasons better, which one tops which benchmark this week, which one handles the longer context window: that is where all the oxygen goes.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Images generated usingIf you can’t read the article further than please click here
Meanwhile, the component that actually decides whether your agent succeeds or flails around in circles sits quietly in the corner of your architecture diagram, unloved and rarely revisited after the day you first picked it.Here is the thing that almost never makes it into the launch tweet. When retrieval is bad, your agent does not simply hand you a worse answer and move on.
It searches again, and then it searches a third time, pulling in irrelevant chunks along the way and reasoning carefully over pure noise. A weak retriever does not just cost you accuracy on the eval set: it costs you real money on every single query, forever, in a line item you probably attribute to the LLM instead.
